channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Research Officer (World Migration Report) – IOM Geneva: Full Opportunity Breakdown
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The International Organization for Migration (IOM), the leading UN agency in the field of migration, has announced a compelling professional opportunity for qualified candidates: Research Officer (World Migration Report), based in Geneva, Switzerland. This role sits within IOM Headquarters and is part of the organization’s competitive mobility exercise for internal and internal‑equivalent candidates. This article […]

The post Research Officer (World Migration Report) – IOM Geneva: Full Opportunity Breakdown appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Organization for Migration (IOM), the leading UN agency in the field of migration, has announced a compelling professional opportunity for qualified candidates: Research Officer (World Migration Report), based in Geneva, Switzerland. This role sits within IOM Headquarters and is part of the organization’s competitive mobility exercise for internal and internal‑equivalent candidates.

This article provides a comprehensive, SEO‑optimized overview of the position, responsibilities, qualifications, competencies, and application details to help potential applicants fully understand the opportunity.

About IOM and the World Migration Report

Established in 1951, IOM promotes humane and orderly migration worldwide. The organization works closely with governments, NGOs, and international partners to save lives, protect migrants, and strengthen migration governance. According to the document, “IOM is committed to fostering a respectful, inclusive and supportive workplace where all employees can thrive professionally and feel valued.”

The World Migration Report (WMR) is IOM’s flagship publication, offering authoritative insights into global migration trends, data, and thematic issues. The Research Officer plays a central role in shaping this influential report.

Position Overview

  • Job Title: Research Officer (World Migration Report)
  • Duty Station: Geneva, Switzerland
  • Contract Type: Fixed-term (1 year, with possibility of extension)
  • Initial Duration: 12 months
  • Grade: P‑2
  • Category: Job Opportunity
  • Application Deadline: 14 July 2026, 16:59 (Geneva local time)
  • Recruitment Type: Professional, internal mobility exercise

Key Responsibilities

The role is deeply embedded in research, editorial coordination, knowledge management, and global communication. As stated in the document, the officer will:

1. Contribute to the Development of the World Migration Report

  • Support the editorial team in designing, preparing, and coordinating the WMR series.
  • Work with internal and external contributors.
  • Draft key sections and co‑author thematic chapters.
  • Conduct data checking and provide additional research support. “Draft key parts of WMRs utilizing relevant data and other sources… participate in the data checking of WMR chapters.”

2. Enhance WMR Digital Tools

  • Contribute to updates of the WMR interactive platform and toolkits.

3. Support Global Dissemination and Outreach

  • Assist with WMR webpage updates, social media content, podcasts, info sheets, translations, media briefings, and launch events.

4. Strengthen Knowledge Management

  • Support development of online tools and webinar series.
  • Encourage use of WMR resources across HQ, Regional Offices, and Country Offices.

5. Provide Technical Guidance

  • Assist regional and country offices in designing and finalizing migration research outputs.

6. Produce High‑Quality Research Materials

  • Write, proofread, edit, and format factsheets, snapshots, articles, and reports.

7. Facilitate Internal Learning

  • Organize knowledge‑sharing activities to strengthen IOM’s research capacities.

8. Undertake Duty Travel

  • Travel as required for WMR promotion and dissemination.

Required Qualifications

Education

  • Master’s degree in Migration Studies, International Relations, Development Studies, Humanitarian Affairs, Political Science, or related fields with two years of experience, OR
  • Bachelor’s degree in the same fields with four years of experience.

Experience

  • Demonstrated experience writing migration‑related papers, briefings, and articles.
  • Experience conducting migration research and presenting findings.
  • Ability to explain complex migration data.
  • Experience collaborating with diverse teams and partners.

Skills

  • Excellent research, writing, analytical, and communication skills.
  • Strong drafting abilities and attention to detail.
  • Ability to work independently and under tight timelines.
  • Understanding of migration issues across geographic contexts.
  • Good computer literacy.

Languages

  • Fluency in English required.
  • Working knowledge of French and/or Spanish is an advantage.

IOM Core Values and Competencies

All IOM staff must demonstrate:

  • Inclusion and respect for diversity
  • Integrity and transparency
  • Professionalism
  • Courage
  • Empathy

Core competencies include teamwork, delivering results, managing knowledge, accountability, and communication.

Important Notes for Applicants

  • Internationally recruited staff must be mobile.
  • Appointment is subject to medical, security, and residency clearances.
  • IOM enforces a strict zero‑tolerance policy on misconduct, including sexual exploitation and abuse.
  • No fees are charged at any stage of recruitment.
  • Applications must be submitted through the IOM WAVE system.
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Deadline

Applications close on 14 July 2026 at 16:59 (Geneva local time).

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more job opportunities here

The post Research Officer (World Migration Report) – IOM Geneva: Full Opportunity Breakdown appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2027 Korea Foundation Support Program for Establishing Professorships in Korean Studies and Korean Language
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Korea Foundation (KF) has announced its 2027 Support for Establishment of Professorship program, designed to strengthen Korean Studies and Korean Language education at universities around the world. This initiative provides substantial financial assistance to institutions seeking to create new professorships, ensuring long-term academic growth and global engagement with Korea-related scholarship. Position (TTP)** The KF […]

The post 2027 Korea Foundation Support Program for Establishing Professorships in Korean Studies and Korean Language appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Korea Foundation (KF) has announced its 2027 Support for Establishment of Professorship program, designed to strengthen Korean Studies and Korean Language education at universities around the world. This initiative provides substantial financial assistance to institutions seeking to create new professorships, ensuring long-term academic growth and global engagement with Korea-related scholarship.

Position (TTP)** The KF covers up to 70% of the total salary and employee benefits for a newly hired professor in Korean Studies or Korean Language.

  • Salary: Based on the university’s existing salary scale.
  • Employee benefits: Includes legally required non-wage payments such as social welfare contributions and fringe benefits.

2. Endowed Chair Position

The KF provides annual endowment funding during the grant period to establish a permanent professorship. The university must contribute a 50% matching fund, dollar-for-dollar.

Grant Duration

  • Three to five years

Eligible Applicants

  • Universities outside Korea planning to establish new professorships in Korean Studies or Korean Language.

Prerequisites for KF Support

Universities must demonstrate the ability to sustain the professorship after KF support ends, either through internal funding or a jointly created endowment. Additionally, the appointed professor must undergo fair and rigorous evaluation according to the university’s standard procedures for appointment, promotion, and tenure.

Program Timeline

  1. Application Period: July 1, 2026 – September 11, 2026 Deadline: September 11, 2026 at 18:00 KST
  2. Notification of Results: December 2026
  3. Grant Start: 2027–28 or 2028–29 academic year

Required Documents

1. Online Application Form

Must include detailed information on the university’s current Korean Studies status (faculty, courses, library holdings, academic activities).

2. Supplementary Materials

The following documents must be uploaded:

  1. Curriculum for B.A./M.A. programs in Korean Studies or Korean Language (if applicable)
  2. University’s salary scale for professors
  3. CV of the proposed professor (if a candidate is already identified)
  4. CV of the project director
  5. Letter from the Dean supporting the establishment of the professorship

Evaluation Criteria

  1. Program Planning & Commitment
    • Clarity and organization of the proposal
    • Long-term plans for Korean Studies development
  2. Institutional Capacity
    • University reputation and prior achievements in Korean Studies
  3. Budget Soundness
    • Specificity and feasibility of the financial plan
    • Matching fund efforts
  4. Expected Outcomes
    • Contribution to regional diversification of Korean Studies
    • Practicality and impact of proposed goals

Important Reminders

  1. Cancellation of Support KF may cancel support if an agreement cannot be finalized with the university.
  2. Eligibility Requirements for Appointed Professors
    • At least 50% of teaching and research must focus on Korea
    • Research must use Korean-language primary sources
    • Publications must be in English, and preferably also in Korean
    • Korean language proficiency is required
  3. Notification of Changes Universities must promptly inform KF of any project changes.
  4. Revision Requests KF may request modifications or additions to the project plan.
  5. Grant Disbursement Funds are released after the grant agreement is signed and a qualified professor is hired.
  6. Annual Reporting Universities must submit yearly project reports with signed financial statements.

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more grant opportunities here 

The post 2027 Korea Foundation Support Program for Establishing Professorships in Korean Studies and Korean Language appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Media Foundation for West Africa Opens Applications for the 9th Edition of the Next Generation Investigative Journalism Fellowship
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Media Foundation for West Africa (MFWA) has officially announced the call for applications for the 9th Edition of the Next Generation Investigative Journalism (NGIJ) Fellowship, a prestigious and fully funded three‑month residential program designed to nurture Ghana’s next wave of accountability and anti‑corruption journalists. The fellowship, scheduled to run from September to November 2026, […]

The post The Media Foundation for West Africa Opens Applications for the 9th Edition of the Next Generation Investigative Journalism Fellowship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Media Foundation for West Africa (MFWA) has officially announced the call for applications for the 9th Edition of the Next Generation Investigative Journalism (NGIJ) Fellowship, a prestigious and fully funded three‑month residential program designed to nurture Ghana’s next wave of accountability and anti‑corruption journalists. The fellowship, scheduled to run from September to November 2026, aims to strengthen investigative reporting, promote transparency, and enhance press freedom across Ghana.

Overview of the Fellowship

The NGIJ Fellowship is a flagship initiative under MFWA’s broader project titled “Countering corruption through accountability journalism and improved freedom of expression environment in Ghana.” This project forms part of the Participation, Accountability, Integrity for a Resilient Democracy (PAIReD) programme, commissioned by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), co‑financed by the European Union (EU) and the Swiss State Secretariat for Economic Affairs (SECO), and implemented by GIZ in cooperation with Ghana’s Ministry of Finance.

The overarching goal is to empower young journalists and media professionals with the skills, tools, and mentorship needed to produce impactful investigative stories that expose corruption, address socio‑economic inequalities, and strengthen democratic governance.

What Participants Will Gain

The fellowship offers a comprehensive blend of training, mentorship, and practical newsroom experience. Selected fellows will benefit from:

  1. Intensive Training Modules
    • Anti‑corruption and accountability reporting
    • Use of Ghana’s Right to Information (RTI) Law
    • Data journalism techniques
    • Fact‑checking methodologies
    • Additional investigative reporting tools and frameworks
  2. Hands‑On Mentorship Fellows will work directly with MFWA’s Independent Journalism Projects:
    • Fact‑Check Ghana
    • The Fourth Estate These platforms are known for producing high‑impact investigative stories and fact‑checking reports that shape national discourse.
  3. Story Production Support
    • Financial assistance to pursue investigative or in‑depth stories
    • Editorial guidance from seasoned investigative journalists
    • Opportunity to produce at least one major story and contribute to a collaborative group investigation
  4. Certification and Stipend
    • A Certificate of Honour upon successful completion
    • Monthly stipend throughout the three‑month residential program

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following criteria:

  1. Must be a journalism graduate, early‑career journalist, or young professional with a strong passion for journalism.
  2. Must commit fully to the residential program from September to November 2026.
  3. Female applicants and journalists residing outside the Greater Accra Region are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Process

 

  1. Motivation Letter (Maximum 400 words) explaining your interest in the fellowship.
  2. Two Published Articles
    • For journalists: print, TV, radio, or online articles
    • For non‑journalist professionals: a published blog post
  3. Letter of Consent from employer (for staff journalists).
  4. Curriculum Vitae (CV) with two referees.

Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

 

For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here

Discover more fellowship opportunities here

The post The Media Foundation for West Africa Opens Applications for the 9th Edition of the Next Generation Investigative Journalism Fellowship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Horowitz Foundation for Social Policy: Comprehensive Guide to the 2024–2025 Grant Application Process
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Horowitz Foundation for Social Policy stands as a long‑established supporter of exceptional social policy research, offering competitive grants that empower scholars to pursue innovative, impactful projects. Since 1997, the Foundation has funded research that advances understanding of pressing social issues and contributes meaningfully to policy development. This detailed guide breaks down the full application […]

The post The Horowitz Foundation for Social Policy: Comprehensive Guide to the 2024–2025 Grant Application Process appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Horowitz Foundation for Social Policy stands as a long‑established supporter of exceptional social policy research, offering competitive grants that empower scholars to pursue innovative, impactful projects. Since 1997, the Foundation has funded research that advances understanding of pressing social issues and contributes meaningfully to policy development. This detailed guide breaks down the full application process, requirements, deadlines, and expectations to help applicants prepare a strong submission.

Overview of the Horowitz Foundation Grant Program

The Foundation provides financial support to emerging and established researchers whose work demonstrates originality, policy relevance, and academic rigor. Each year, applicants from diverse disciplines submit proposals addressing critical social challenges, ranging from inequality and governance to public health, education, and community development.

The grant program is administered entirely through an online portal, ensuring a streamlined and accessible application experience. Award recipients are announced in June each year, following a thorough review process conducted by the Foundation’s board and evaluators.

Application Steps: Full Breakdown

The application process consists of several structured components designed to evaluate both the researcher and the proposed project. Applicants must complete each step carefully to ensure eligibility.

1. Create an Account in the Application Portal

Applicants begin by clicking the Application Form button on the Foundation’s website. This leads to the secure application portal, where users must create an account. The portal allows applicants to save progress, upload documents, and track submission status.

2. Complete All Required Application Sections

The application includes multiple sections, each serving a specific purpose in evaluating the applicant’s qualifications and project potential.

A. Personal Details

This section consists of short‑answer questions that help reviewers understand the applicant’s background, academic trajectory, and motivations. Applicants should provide clear, concise responses that highlight relevant experience and interests.

B. Project Overview

A brief snapshot of the proposed research project. This overview should summarize the core idea, research question, and anticipated contribution to social policy.

C. Full Project Description (750 Words)

This is the most substantial part of the application. Applicants must submit a detailed description of their project, up to 750 words. Strong project descriptions typically include:

  • Research question and significance
  • Theoretical framework or conceptual foundation
  • Methodology and data sources
  • Expected outcomes and policy relevance
  • Timeline and feasibility

D. Upload Your CV

Applicants must upload a current curriculum vitae that outlines academic achievements, publications, research experience, and other relevant credentials.

E. Provide Contact Information for Recommenders

Applicants must list the contact details of recommenders who will submit formal recommendation forms. These individuals should be familiar with the applicant’s academic or professional work.

3. Review & Submit the Application

Once all sections are complete, applicants must click the Review & Submit button. This final step must be completed on or before the deadline: December 1 at 11:59 PM EST.

The portal allows applicants to review all entries before submission to ensure accuracy and completeness.

4. Ensure Recommenders Submit Their Forms

Recommendation forms must also be submitted by December 1 at 11:59 PM EST. Applications with missing recommendations will not be reviewed.

Applicants are encouraged to notify recommenders early and follow up as the deadline approaches.

Additional Important Information

To maintain fairness and consistency, the Foundation enforces several strict guidelines:

  • All applications must be submitted through the official online portal.
  • All forms and documents must be written in English.
  • Incomplete applications will not be considered.
  • Applications cannot be reviewed if recommendation forms are missing.
  • Award winners will be announced in June.
  • Applicants can join the Foundation’s email list to receive updates on deadlines, announcements, and opportunities.

 

For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here

Discover more  grant opportunities here

 

The post The Horowitz Foundation for Social Policy: Comprehensive Guide to the 2024–2025 Grant Application Process appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Call for Projects – UEFA Foundation for Children (2026 Edition)
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The UEFA Foundation for Children has officially opened its Call for Projects 2026, inviting organisations and NGOs across the globe to submit impactful initiatives that improve the lives of vulnerable children. This annual call, held throughout June and July, is one of the foundation’s most important mechanisms for advancing children’s rights, strengthening communities, and promoting […]

The post Call for Projects – UEFA Foundation for Children (2026 Edition) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UEFA Foundation for Children has officially opened its Call for Projects 2026, inviting organisations and NGOs across the globe to submit impactful initiatives that improve the lives of vulnerable children. This annual call, held throughout June and July, is one of the foundation’s most important mechanisms for advancing children’s rights, strengthening communities, and promoting sustainable development through sport and social inclusion.

The foundation’s mission is rooted in solidarity, ethical values, and development aid. Its support extends far beyond Europe, embracing global organisations that share its commitment to protecting and empowering children.

Purpose of the Call for Projects

The UEFA Foundation for Children aims to identify and support innovative, practical, and ethically grounded projects that directly benefit children facing vulnerability, exclusion, or hardship. Through this initiative, the foundation promotes:

  • Fundamental rights of children
  • Access to safe environments
  • Social inclusion
  • Long-term community development

Organisations that apply must demonstrate a clear alignment with the foundation’s values and propose tangible solutions that create measurable impact.

Key Focus Areas

Applicants may submit projects that address one or more of the following priority areas:

  1. Access to Sport Projects that provide children with opportunities to participate in sports, regardless of background or ability.
  2. Personal Development Initiatives that support emotional, social, and educational growth.
  3. Disabled Children Programs designed to ensure inclusion, accessibility, and equal opportunities for children with disabilities.
  4. Healthy Lifestyle Activities promoting physical health, nutrition, and overall well-being.
  5. Conflict Victims Support for children affected by war, displacement, or violence.
  6. Gender Equality Projects that empower girls and promote equal access to opportunities.
  7. Environmental Protection Child-focused environmental education and sustainability initiatives.
  8. Employability Programs that equip older children and youth with skills for future employment.
  9. Infrastructure and Equipment Construction, renovation, or provision of safe spaces, sports facilities, or essential equipment.
  10. Strengthening Partnerships Collaborative initiatives that unite multiple organisations for greater impact.

Selection Process

Every submitted project undergoes a thorough evaluation by the UEFA Foundation for Children. The Board of Trustees reviews all proposals and makes final selections during its autumn meeting, following the foundation’s statutes and ethical guidelines.

Projects must meet strict criteria related to:

  • Ethical compliance
  • Feasibility
  • Sustainability
  • Impact measurement
  • Alignment with the foundation’s mission

Application Platform

The Candidate Registration Platform is now open for the 2026 Call for Projects. Eligible organisations are encouraged to prepare early and submit their proposals before the closing date.

Application Deadline: 3 August 2026.

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more grant  opportunities here

 

The post Call for Projects – UEFA Foundation for Children (2026 Edition) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Empowering the Next Generation of Environmental Leaders: Inside the Joke Waller‑Hunter Initiative (JWHI)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Joke Waller‑Hunter Initiative (JWHI), powered by Both ENDS, stands as one of the most influential global programs dedicated to nurturing young environmental leaders in developing countries. Through targeted small grants, JWHI unlocks the potential of emerging civil society champions, enabling them to strengthen their skills, expand their knowledge, and elevate the impact of environmental […]

The post Empowering the Next Generation of Environmental Leaders: Inside the Joke Waller‑Hunter Initiative (JWHI) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Joke Waller‑Hunter Initiative (JWHI), powered by Both ENDS, stands as one of the most influential global programs dedicated to nurturing young environmental leaders in developing countries. Through targeted small grants, JWHI unlocks the potential of emerging civil society champions, enabling them to strengthen their skills, expand their knowledge, and elevate the impact of environmental organizations worldwide.

A Mission Rooted in Youth Empowerment and Environmental Justice

JWHI was established to honor Joke Waller‑Hunter’s legacy of environmental stewardship and global sustainability. Today, the Initiative continues her vision by investing in young individuals who are already driving change within their communities. These grants are designed to support personal development rather than organizational projects, ensuring that young leaders gain the tools and confidence needed to influence environmental policy, advocacy, and action.

What JWHI Does

The Initiative focuses on strengthening the capacity and efficiency of environmental Civil Society Organisations (CSOs) by empowering the young people who lead them. JWHI achieves this through:

  • Small Grants for Personal Development: These grants support activities such as training programs, courses, internships, conferences, and other learning opportunities that enhance leadership and environmental expertise.
  • Capacity Building for Environmental CSOs: By investing in individuals, JWHI indirectly strengthens the organizations they serve, improving long‑term sustainability and impact.
  • Opportunities for Growth and Exposure: Grantees gain access to global networks, new skills, and platforms that amplify their voices and initiatives.

Meet the Changemakers: JWHI Grantees

JWHI proudly showcases a diverse group of young environmental champions who are transforming their communities:

  1. Scovia Ampumuza – Rwanda Founder of Faraja Rwanda, Scovia advocates for fair, inclusive, and gender‑responsive climate policy.
  2. Alab Mirasol Ayroso – Philippines National coordinator of Youth Advocates for Climate Action Philippines, Alab mobilizes youth voices in climate justice.
  3. Maria Reyes – Mexico A climate activist leveraging journalism and communication to raise awareness and fight the climate crisis.

These stories highlight the Initiative’s commitment to uplifting young leaders who are shaping environmental futures across continents.

How to Get Involved

JWHI invites passionate young environmental leaders to apply for its grants and take the next step in their personal and professional development. The Initiative supports individuals pursuing:

  • Courses and academic training
  • Internships and mentorship programs
  • Conferences and global environmental events
  • Organizational capacity‑building activities
  • Skill‑enhancement programs

Deadline: The 2026 call is now open. Applications close on 31st August 2026.

Why Apply for a JWHI Grant?

  • Strengthen your leadership skills
  • Gain access to global environmental networks
  • Enhance your organization’s impact
  • Receive tailored support for your personal development
  • Join a community of young changemakers across the world

Powered by Both ENDS

JWHI operates under Both ENDS, an organization committed to environmental justice and sustainable development. Together, they champion youth leadership, climate justice, and equitable environmental governance.

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more grant opportunities here

 

The post Empowering the Next Generation of Environmental Leaders: Inside the Joke Waller‑Hunter Initiative (JWHI) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Net Zero Challenge 2026 — A Global Call for Climate Innovators
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Net Zero Challenge 2026 — A Global Call for Climate Innovators The Net Zero Challenge (N0C) 2026 is a global climate‑technology innovation competition designed to accelerate groundbreaking solutions that can help Vietnam—and the world—combat climate change. Organized by Touchstone Partners and Temasek Foundation, this initiative seeks early‑stage innovators, researchers, entrepreneurs, and visionary teams capable of […]

The post Net Zero Challenge 2026 — A Global Call for Climate Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Net Zero Challenge 2026 — A Global Call for Climate Innovators

The Net Zero Challenge (N0C) 2026 is a global climate‑technology innovation competition designed to accelerate groundbreaking solutions that can help Vietnam—and the world—combat climate change. Organized by Touchstone Partners and Temasek Foundation, this initiative seeks early‑stage innovators, researchers, entrepreneurs, and visionary teams capable of reshaping environmental sustainability at scale.

Application Deadline: 11:59 PM, 31 August 2026 (GMT+7)

Zero Challenge is more than a competition; it is a global platform that empowers climate‑tech pioneers to showcase transformative ideas. The program identifies and supports innovations that can significantly reduce emissions, improve environmental resilience, and accelerate Vietnam’s journey toward net‑zero goals.

The competition is structured to ensure clarity, fairness, and depth of evaluation. Applicants must complete a four‑part application form that captures the full scope of their innovation.

Four-Part Application Structure

1. General Registration

This section collects essential information about the applicant or team. It ensures that organizers can verify eligibility, contact participants, and understand the foundational background of each submission.

2. Project Overview

Applicants must provide a comprehensive description of their climate‑tech innovation. This includes:

  • Problem Statement — What environmental challenge does the project address?
  • Solution Description — How does the innovation work?
  • Impact Potential — How can the solution scale to benefit Vietnam and beyond?
  • Uniqueness — What makes this innovation different from existing solutions?

3. Pilot Plan

This section focuses on real‑world implementation. Applicants must outline:

  • Pilot Objectives
  • Pilot Location (if applicable)
  • Timeline and Milestones
  • Required Resources
  • Expected Outcomes

The pilot plan demonstrates feasibility and readiness for deployment.

4. Supporting Information

Applicants may include additional context, research, or data. However, all critical information must be written directly in the application form—not as external attachments such as videos, images, or links.

Submission & Evaluation

  • Only submitted applications are eligible for evaluation.
  • Applicants may save progress and return to complete the form anytime before the deadline.
  • Submitted materials will be shared with the shortlisting committee and judges panel.
  • Finalists will deliver a publicly livestreamed pitch, and by applying, participants consent to this public disclosure.
  • Confidential information will be protected and used solely for evaluation, except during finalist pitches.

 

For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here

Discover more  competition opportunities here 

 

The post Net Zero Challenge 2026 — A Global Call for Climate Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AGFUND Development Programme: A Transformative Six‑Month Opportunity for Young African Changemakers
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The AGFUND Development Programme is a groundbreaking six‑month pilot initiative created through a powerful partnership between AGFUND and The Diana Award. Designed exclusively for Diana Award Recipients across Africa, this programme aims to strengthen, sustain, and scale the social action projects led by young changemakers who are already making a measurable difference in their communities. […]

The post AGFUND Development Programme: A Transformative Six‑Month Opportunity for Young African Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The AGFUND Development Programme is a groundbreaking six‑month pilot initiative created through a powerful partnership between AGFUND and The Diana Award. Designed exclusively for Diana Award Recipients across Africa, this programme aims to strengthen, sustain, and scale the social action projects led by young changemakers who are already making a measurable difference in their communities.

This initiative responds directly to the needs expressed by Diana Award Recipients across the continent—particularly the need for practical support in sustainability, leadership, partnerships, funding, and long‑term growth. Through expert-led learning, peer collaboration, and personalised development support, participants gain the tools and networks required to elevate their impact.

Programme Overview

The AGFUND Development Programme offers participants access to a vibrant community of fellow changemakers, expert facilitators, and development professionals. The programme is structured to support both personal growth and the long-term success of each participant’s social action work.

Key Features of the Programme

  • Practical, hands-on learning experiences
  • Collaborative sessions with peers across Africa
  • Personalised development conversations
  • Access to expert-led workshops and mentorship
  • Opportunities to strengthen leadership and project sustainability
  • A supportive community committed to long-term impact

Who Is the Programme For?

This programme is designed specifically for Diana Award Recipients based in Africa who are already leading social action initiatives. It is ideal for young leaders seeking structured guidance, strategic support, and opportunities to grow their projects beyond their current reach.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants will benefit from a wide range of developmental opportunities, including:

  1. Strengthened Leadership Skills Through expert-led sessions, participants will refine their leadership abilities and learn how to manage teams, partnerships, and community engagement more effectively.
  2. Sustainability and Growth Strategies The programme provides practical guidance on sustaining long-term projects, securing funding, and scaling initiatives to reach more beneficiaries.
  3. Peer Collaboration and Networking Participants join a continent-wide network of changemakers, fostering collaboration, shared learning, and long-term relationships.
  4. Personalised Development Support Each participant receives tailored guidance through one-on-one development conversations during onboarding and throughout the programme.

Programme Topics

The programme covers a wide range of essential topics, including:

  • Sustainability and long-term project planning
  • Leadership development
  • Partnership building
  • Funding strategies and grant readiness
  • Scaling social impact
  • Community engagement
  • Monitoring and evaluation
  • Personal growth and resilience

How the Programme Works

The AGFUND Development Programme is structured to be deeply practical and collaborative. Participants engage in:

  • Live virtual sessions led by experts
  • Peer learning circles
  • Personalised development conversations
  • Practical assignments and real-world application
  • Community-building activities
  • Ongoing support from programme facilitators

What Makes This Opportunity Unique?

Practical & Collaborative Learning

The programme is grounded in real-world experiences and challenges faced by young African changemakers. Every session is designed to be actionable and relevant.

Supporting Recipients Beyond Recognition

The Diana Award remains committed to supporting its recipients long after their initial recognition. This programme is a direct extension of that commitment.

Fostering Connection Across Africa

By bringing together recipients from across the continent, the programme strengthens regional networks and creates opportunities for collaboration and shared impact.

Application Process

To apply for the AGFUND Development Programme, applicants must:

  1. Review the full eligibility criteria and FAQs
  2. Complete the application form by Friday, 31 July 2026 (23:59 GMT)
  3. Await selection—up to 25 participants will be chosen for Cohort 1
  4. Participate in onboarding activities in September and October 2026
  5. Attend the programme induction session and initial one-on-one development conversation

A second cohort is expected to begin in January 2027. Applicants who are not selected for Cohort 1 may be considered for future opportunities.

Time Commitment for Participants

Participants should expect to engage in regular virtual sessions, peer learning activities, and development conversations throughout the six-month programme. The commitment is designed to be manageable for young leaders balancing school, work, or ongoing community projects.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the AGFUND Development Programme?

A six-month pilot initiative supporting Diana Award Recipients across Africa to strengthen and scale their social action work.

Who Can Apply?

Diana Award Recipients based in Africa who are already leading social action projects.

Is There a Cost to Participate?

No, participation is fully funded.

How Many Participants Will Be Selected?

Up to 25 participants for Cohort 1.

Can I Apply If I Only Have an Idea?

No, applicants must already be leading an active project.

What Topics Will the Programme Cover?

Leadership, sustainability, partnerships, funding, growth, and more.

When Does the Programme Start?

October 2026.

What Happens During Onboarding?

Participants join induction sessions and have an initial one-on-one development conversation.

Will There Be Interviews?

Selection is based on eligibility, motivation, programme fit, and availability.

Will I Receive a Certificate?

Yes, participants will receive a certificate upon completion.

What Platform Will Be Used for Sessions?

Virtual platforms will be used; details will be shared during onboarding.

Can I Apply Again If Unsuccessful?

Yes, applicants may be considered for future cohorts.

Made Possible By

This programme is supported by AGFUND and delivered by The Diana Award, whose mission is to inspire and develop positive change in the lives of young people.

Deadline: Apply by 31 July 2026 (23:59 GMT)

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more  fellowship opportunities here

The post AGFUND Development Programme: A Transformative Six‑Month Opportunity for Young African Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Resilient Coders Fall 2026 Fellowship: Apply for a Free Full Stack Engineering Bootcamp and Launch Your Software Engineering Career
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Resilient Coders is now accepting applications for its Fall 2026 Full Stack Engineering Fellowship, a fully funded, intensive software engineering training programme designed to prepare talented individuals from underrepresented backgrounds for high-growth careers in technology. The fellowship is completely free of charge and offers participants an opportunity to gain practical software engineering skills, receive mentorship […]

The post Resilient Coders Fall 2026 Fellowship: Apply for a Free Full Stack Engineering Bootcamp and Launch Your Software Engineering Career appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Resilient Coders is now accepting applications for its Fall 2026 Full Stack Engineering Fellowship, a fully funded, intensive software engineering training programme designed to prepare talented individuals from underrepresented backgrounds for high-growth careers in technology.

The fellowship is completely free of charge and offers participants an opportunity to gain practical software engineering skills, receive mentorship from experienced professionals, collaborate with peers, and build a strong foundation for employment in the tech industry.

For over a decade, Resilient Coders has helped participants launch successful careers at leading companies such as Audible, CarGurus, Accenture, Liberty Mutual, and many other technology-focused employers.

Interested applicants must first submit an Interest Form to gain access to the official Fall 2026 application.

The extended application deadline is July 10, 2026.

About the Resilient Coders Fellowship

Resilient Coders is committed to creating opportunities for individuals from underrepresented communities to access rewarding careers in software engineering.

The Full Stack Engineering Fellowship provides participants with intensive technical training while helping them develop professional skills, confidence, and industry connections.

The programme combines:

  • Live instructor-led training
  • Hands-on coding projects
  • Team collaboration
  • Professional mentorship
  • Career preparation
  • Networking opportunities
  • Portfolio development

Graduates leave the programme equipped with practical software engineering skills and the experience necessary to pursue employment in the technology sector.

Fellowship Benefits

Participants selected for the Fall 2026 cohort will receive numerous benefits, including:

  • Free Full Stack Engineering training
  • Live instruction from experienced software engineers
  • Remote learning from anywhere within the eligible U.S. regions
  • Mentorship from industry professionals
  • Community support throughout the programme
  • Collaborative coding experience
  • Hands-on software development projects
  • Interview preparation
  • Career readiness support
  • Networking opportunities with technology professionals
  • Opportunity to join a growing alumni network
  • Increased access to careers at top technology companies

Over the past ten years, Resilient Coders alumni have secured employment at organizations including:

  • Audible
  • CarGurus
  • Accenture
  • Liberty Mutual
  • And many other leading employers.

Programme Schedule

The Fall 2026 Fellowship follows a structured full-time schedule.

Programme Dates

August 3, 2026 – December 18, 2026

Duration

  • 18 weeks
  • Full-time
  • Fully remote

Class Schedule

Monday – Friday

10:00 AM – 3:00 PM (EST)

Community Mentor Sessions

Tuesdays and Thursdays

3:30 PM – 6:00 PM (EST)

Applicants should note that additional programme expectations exist beyond the scheduled class hours.

Who Can Apply?

Priority consideration is given to applicants who:

  • Are between 18 and 35 years old.
  • Come from low-income households based on HUD guidelines.
  • Are passionate about software engineering.
  • Are ready to commit to an intensive learning experience.
  • Are eager to build a long-term career in technology.

Individuals over the age of 35 are still encouraged to apply.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Be authorized to work in the United States.
  • Live in:
    • Greater Boston
    • Greater Philadelphia
    • Maine
    • New York
  • Hold at least a High School Diploma or GED.
  • Not possess a Master’s degree or higher.
  • Meet the programme’s low-income eligibility requirements.
  • Commit to the full programme schedule.

Application Process

Applicants should carefully complete each stage of the selection process.

Step 1: Watch the Introduction Video

Prospective applicants begin by watching the one-hour HTML introduction video.

Step 2: Submit an Interest Form

Submitting the Interest Form allows applicants to:

  • Receive recruitment updates.
  • Access the official application.
  • Receive important reminders and deadlines.

Applicants are encouraged to monitor their email after submitting the Interest Form.

Step 3: Complete the Official Application

Applicants should prepare:

  • An updated resume.
  • A LinkedIn profile.

Applications must be completed before the deadline.

Step 4: Complete the Responsive Web Design Course

Applicants are encouraged to complete the Responsive Web Design course available on freeCodeCamp.

This course provides foundational knowledge that prepares participants for success during the fellowship.

Step 5: Participate in a Hackathon

Eligible applicants will receive an invitation to participate in a required coding hackathon.

During the hackathon, participants will:

  • Work in teams.
  • Build a website.
  • Collaborate with alumni facilitators.
  • Demonstrate coding skills.
  • Learn about the student experience.

Participation in the hackathon is a mandatory part of the application process.

Step 6: Interview

Semi-finalists will be invited to participate in an interview with a member of the Resilient Coders programme team.

Step 7: Paperwork and Onboarding

Finalists will submit documentation verifying:

  • Age
  • Address
  • Financial eligibility
  • Employment authorization

Following successful onboarding, participants will prepare to begin classes.

Why Choose Resilient Coders?

The fellowship offers much more than technical instruction.

Participants gain:

  • Real-world coding experience
  • Professional mentorship
  • Industry exposure
  • Career coaching
  • Collaborative learning
  • Access to a strong alumni network
  • Skills aligned with employer needs

By the end of the programme, graduates are well positioned to pursue careers as software engineers and developers across the technology industry.

Important Dates

Applicants should note the following timeline:

  • Application Opens: April 1, 2026
  • Extended Application Deadline: July 10, 2026
  • Programme Begins: August 3, 2026
  • Programme Ends: December 18, 2026

How to Apply

Interested applicants must first submit the Interest Form to receive access to the official application for the Fall 2026 Fellowship.

After submitting the Interest Form, applicants should regularly check their email for instructions from the Resilient Coders admissions team.

For additional information and to begin your application, visit the official Resilient Coders website.

Application Process

  1. Watch our 1 hour long introduction the HTML video
  2. Submit an Interest Form
    ‍Sign up to get access to the application and to receive recruitment alerts about the application process.
  3. Complete the Application before the deadline
    You will need a resume and LinkedIn to complete the application.
  4. ‍Start working on the Responsive Web Design Courseon freeCodeCamp
    This course is meant to provide you with a strong base to hit the ground running on the first day of classes.
  5. Code With Us
    If you meet our eligibility criteria, you will be invited to participate in one of our hackathons. Attending a hackathon is a required step of the application process. You’ll get a chance to work with a group facilitated by one of our program alumni to create a website. This is an opportunity to test your coding skills while learning more about the Resilient Coders student experience.
  6. Schedule an Interview
    Semi-finalists will be will be invited to schedule an interview call with a program team member.
  7. Paperwork & Onboarding
    Finalists will be asked to submit documentation verifying your age, address, financial statement, and employment eligibility.
  8. Get ready to start classes!

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Resilient Coders Fall 2026 Fellowship: Apply for a Free Full Stack Engineering Bootcamp and Launch Your Software Engineering Career appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GIZ South Africa is Hiring! Apply for Three Exciting Career Opportunities under the CPD4E Project Before July 2026
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) has announced three exciting career opportunities under its Career Path Development for Employment (CPD4E) Project, providing experienced professionals with the chance to contribute to South Africa’s employment creation and Just Energy Transition agenda. Implemented in partnership with the Department of Higher Education and Training (DHET), the CPD4E project […]

The post GIZ South Africa is Hiring! Apply for Three Exciting Career Opportunities under the CPD4E Project Before July 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) has announced three exciting career opportunities under its Career Path Development for Employment (CPD4E) Project, providing experienced professionals with the chance to contribute to South Africa’s employment creation and Just Energy Transition agenda.

Implemented in partnership with the Department of Higher Education and Training (DHET), the CPD4E project focuses on improving employment opportunities for young South Africans by strengthening skills development, supporting enterprise growth, and preparing the workforce for the country’s transition to a decarbonized economy.

The project is currently recruiting qualified candidates for three technical positions that will contribute to employment promotion, skills development, entrepreneurship, and labour market readiness across South Africa.

Interested applicants should note that each vacancy has its own application deadline ranging from 15 July 2026 to 17 July 2026.

About the Career Path Development for Employment (CPD4E) Project

The Career Path Development for Employment (CPD4E) Project is designed to improve the employment prospects of young people in South Africa by aligning education, skills development, and labour market needs within a rapidly changing economy.

Implemented by GIZ in collaboration with the Department of Higher Education and Training (DHET), the programme supports South Africa’s transition toward a greener, more sustainable economy while creating inclusive employment opportunities.

The project focuses on:

  • Improving youth employability.
  • Supporting employment-oriented private sector development.
  • Enhancing Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET).
  • Promoting entrepreneurship and Small, Medium and Micro Enterprises (SMMEs).
  • Supporting the Just Energy Transition through skills development.
  • Strengthening labour market readiness.

Available Positions

1. Senior Technical Advisor: SMME Development and Support (Output 2)

This position will focus on promoting employment through enterprise development by supporting small businesses and encouraging private sector growth.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Supporting employment-oriented private sector development.
  • Promoting entrepreneurship and SMME growth.
  • Developing initiatives that create employment opportunities.
  • Working closely with stakeholders to strengthen business ecosystems.
  • Supporting sustainable economic development initiatives.

2. Senior Technical Advisor: Employment Support through Skills Development (Outputs 3 & 4)

This role supports skills development initiatives aimed at improving employment outcomes across South Africa.

The successful candidate will:

  • Support programme development within the TVET sector.
  • Assist with implementation of new skills programmes.
  • Contribute to the Team Europe Initiative focusing on the battery value chain.
  • Strengthen employment pathways through skills development.
  • Collaborate with education and training stakeholders.

3. Technical Advisor: JET-Skills (Seconded to the Presidential Climate Commission) 

This position supports South Africa’s Just Energy Transition (JET) agenda by strengthening workforce readiness for the country’s evolving economy.

Responsibilities include:

  • Coordinating JET Skills projects.
  • Supporting implementation of the Presidential Climate Commission’s Just Transition agenda.
  • Managing projects related to labour market readiness.
  • Developing employment pathways.
  • Supporting skills development initiatives linked to climate transition priorities.
  • Working closely with government and strategic partners.

Why Work with GIZ?

GIZ is one of the world’s leading international development organizations, working with governments and partners to promote sustainable development worldwide.

Joining the CPD4E project offers professionals the opportunity to:

  • Work on nationally significant employment initiatives.
  • Contribute to South Africa’s Just Energy Transition.
  • Support youth employment and economic development.
  • Collaborate with government institutions and international partners.
  • Gain experience on high-impact development programmes.
  • Build expertise in labour market and skills development initiatives.
  • Join a globally respected development organization.

Application Requirements

Applicants should ensure they submit:

  • A complete application.
  • An updated CV.
  • A motivation letter.

Applications submitted without a motivation letter will not be considered.

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Application Deadlines

Applicants should carefully observe the individual closing dates for each position.

Senior Technical Advisor: SMME Development and Support

Application Deadline: 17 July 2026

Senior Technical Advisor: Employment Support through Skills Development

Application Deadline: 16 July 2026

Technical Advisor: JET-Skills

Application Deadline: 15 July 2026

Early applications are encouraged.

Why You Should Apply

These positions offer an outstanding opportunity to contribute to South Africa’s future workforce by supporting employment, entrepreneurship, skills development, and climate transition initiatives.

Professionals passionate about youth employment, sustainable development, labour market transformation, and public-private collaboration are encouraged to submit their applications before the respective deadlines.

 

 

Learn More

  • More global Jobs opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post GIZ South Africa is Hiring! Apply for Three Exciting Career Opportunities under the CPD4E Project Before July 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
